decks.notes: The main concept of this deck is to use aggressive creatures like Cori-Steel Cutter, Monastery Swiftspear, and Emberheart Challenger to quickly deal damage to the opponent. The deck also utilizes burn spells like Burst Lightning and Reckless Rage to remove threats and finish off the opponent. The deck aims to apply pressure early on and maintain it throughout the game using efficient creatures and removal spells, while also having access to card draw and tempo plays with cards like Academic Dispute and Boomerang Basics.

The deck focuses on assembling the combo of Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to generate a strong board presence and overwhelm the opponent. It includes a mix of creatures, spells, and lands to support the combo and disrupt the opponent's strategy. The deck aims to control the game early on with cards like Thoughtseize and Witherbloom Command, while setting up the combo pieces for a powerful finish. By utilizing the synergy between Greasefang and Parhelion II, the deck seeks to establish dominance and secure victory in a Pioneer 1 vs 1 match.

The main concept of this UR Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with fast and efficient creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Soul-Scar Mage, backed up by powerful burn spells like Reckless Rage and Fiery Impulse. The deck aims to control the board with removal spells like Boomerang Basics and tempo plays with cards like Stock Up and Vivi Ornitier. The inclusion of cards like Experimental Synthesizer and Stormchaser's Talent allows for additional card advantage and pump spells to finish off the game quickly. Overall, the deck focuses on aggressive plays and efficient spells to maintain pressure on the opponent.

The main concept of this deck is to use efficient and cheap spells to quickly deal damage to the opponent while maintaining card advantage through card draw and tempo plays. By utilizing a combination of burn spells and aggressive creatures, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent before they can stabilize or mount a defense. The inclusion of cards like Academic Dispute and Riverpyre Verge helps to generate additional value and ensure that the deck can maintain pressure throughout the game.

This deck focuses on utilizing Arclight Phoenix as a key win condition by quickly filling up the graveyard with cheap spells and then bringing it back from the graveyard for repeated attacks. The deck also includes cards like Consider and Opt to dig through the deck for more spells, while using cards like Fiery Impulse and Lightning Axe to control the board and clear the way for the Phoenix. Additionally, cards like Cori-Steel Cutter and Picklock Prankster provide additional aggressive threats to overwhelm the opponent. The deck is designed for a fast-paced, aggressive playstyle aiming to quickly close out games.

The deck focuses on assembling the Greasefang Parhelion combo, utilizing cards like Greasefang, Okiba Boss and Parhelion II to create a powerful synergy. It also includes support cards like Fatal Push and Thoughtseize to control the board and disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck is designed to quickly assemble its combo pieces and generate advantage in order to overwhelm the opponent and secure victory. By efficiently utilizing its resources and strategic planning, the deck aims to outmaneuver and outplay its opponents in a fast-paced Pioneer 1 vs 1 format.

This deck focuses on assembling the combo of Greasefang and Parhelion II to generate powerful tokens and overwhelm the opponent. It uses cards like Fleeting Spirit and Monument to Endurance to support the combo and protect key pieces. The deck also includes disruption elements like Thoughtseize and Fatal Push to control the game and ensure the combo can be executed successfully. By utilizing a mix of creatures, lands, and removal spells, the deck aims to create a strong board presence and dominate the battlefield.

The main concept of this deck is to aggressively control the game by using low-cost spells and creatures with the potential to deal direct damage to the opponent. The deck focuses on quickly establishing a board presence and maintaining pressure through efficient removal spells and versatile creatures. By utilizing a mix of burn spells, prowess triggers, and card draw effects, the deck aims to consistently outpace the opponent and secure a victory through a combination of direct damage and combat prowess.

This UW Control deck focuses on card advantage, board control, and disruption through counterspells and removal. The deck utilizes Kaheera as a companion for additional value. The deck includes a variety of utility lands and powerful spells like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and Supreme Verdict to outvalue and outmaneuver opponents. The control elements of the deck are supplemented by card draw and filtering effects to ensure consistency and reach the necessary resources to answer threats and stabilize the board. By maintaining a careful balance of reactive and proactive elements, the deck aims to control the game and emerge victorious.

The main concept of this Selesnya Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of early aggressive creatures and disruptive spells. By utilizing cards like Collected Company and Skyclave Apparition, the deck aims to apply constant pressure on the opponent while also controlling the board. Thalia, Guardian of Thraben acts as a disruptive force to slow down the opponent's game plan, while Elspeth, Storm's Slayer provides a powerful late-game threat. Overall, the strategy is to quickly build a board presence and maintain pressure throughout the game.

This deck focuses on ramping up mana quickly with cards like Lotus Field and Arboreal Grazer, allowing the player to cast big spells like Emergent Ultimatum and Omniscience. Vizier of Tumbling Sands and Hidden Strings are used to untap Lotus Field and generate even more mana. The deck also includes ways to search for key cards like Sylvan Scrying and Bala Ged Recovery. In addition, the deck features removal spells like Fatal Push and Go for the Throat to control the board. Overall, the goal is to set up a powerful combo quickly and overwhelm the opponent.

This deck focuses on using a mix of removal spells, hand disruption, and powerful creatures to control the game and outvalue the opponent. The key strategy is to disrupt your opponent's game plan with cards like Thoughtseize and Duress, while using removal spells like Fatal Push and Abrupt Decay to deal with threats. The deck also utilizes powerful creatures like Sheoldred, the Apocalypse and Liliana of the Veil to generate advantage and close out the game. By controlling the board and hand, the deck aims to dictate the pace of the game and come out on top.

The deck focuses on controlling the board and opponent's actions through countermagic, removal, and card advantage. It aims to stabilize the game with efficient removal spells like Supreme Verdict, counterspells like Dovin's Veto, and card draw like Memory Deluge. The inclusion of the companion Kaheera, the Orphanguard provides additional value and utility to the deck. The mana base is carefully constructed to ensure smooth color fixing and consistent access to the necessary spells. Overall, the deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver opponents through careful resource management and control.

The main focus of this UR Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with fast and efficient creatures while also disrupting their game plan with targeted removal spells. By utilizing cards like Soul-Scar Mage, Monastery Swiftspear, and Stormchaser's Talent, the deck aims to deal damage quickly and efficiently. Key cards like Reckless Rage and Fiery Impulse allow for removal of threats on the opponent's side of the board, while cards like Stock Up and Academic Dispute provide card advantage and flexibility in gameplay. The deck aims to apply early pressure and maintain control throughout the game.

This Selesnya Aggro deck focuses on leveraging lower-cost creatures to quickly build a strong board presence and apply pressure on the opponent. By utilizing mana dorks like Elvish Mystic and Llanowar Elves, along with efficient creatures like Skyclave Apparition and Archon of Emeria, the deck aims to control the board while quickly deploying threats. Collected Company provides additional creature options and card advantage. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben to slow down opponents. Overall, the strategy revolves around fast, efficient creature plays to overwhelm the opponent and secure victory.

This deck focuses on controlling the opponent's plays and maximizing value through the use of Niv-Mizzet Reborn's ability to draw cards and bring powerful multicolored spells to the hand. The inclusion of cards like Bring to Light, Maelstrom Pulse, and Supreme Verdict allows for versatile removal options, while the companion Yorion, Sky Nomad provides additional value through blinking permanents. The deck also includes a variety of lands to ensure consistent mana fixing and ramp, further supporting the control game plan.
